Adam Penn

Engineer turned founder. I build and invest in real assets with a bias toward long-term thinking, hands-on operations, and financial freedom.

Investing Bidwell Capital Fund Helping investors build wealth through real assets: short-term rental funds, oil syndications, and tax-advantaged strategies. Visit Operations Penn Properties Vacation rental management in Northern California. Hands-on operations, great guest experiences, consistent returns. Visit Media Wealth Independence Podcast Conversations on passive income, alternative investments, and building financial freedom, especially for tech professionals. Visit
What I'm Building

I spent the first chapter of my career writing software. It taught me how to think in systems, debug problems under pressure, and build things that actually work. Eventually I realized I wanted to apply that same mindset to something more tangible, so I started buying real estate.

In 2017, I put $23,000 into my first property. Over the next several years, I used strategic debt and reinvested gains to grow that into a portfolio worth over $1.6 million. Along the way, I built the management company to run it, launched an investment fund to help others access similar strategies, and started a podcast about the whole process.

I'm not chasing scale for its own sake. I'm building toward independence, the kind that comes from owning what you operate and aligning your work with how you want to live.

Principles
  • Think in decades, not quarters. The best returns come from patience and compounding.
  • Own the operations. If you don't understand how the machine works, you don't really own it.
  • Freedom is the real metric. Revenue means nothing if you've built yourself a cage.
  • Stay close to the asset. Real wealth comes from real things, not abstractions.
  • Build systems, not just deals. A good system outlasts any single opportunity.
  • Say no more than you say yes. Discipline is a competitive advantage.
  • Integrity compounds too. How you operate matters as much as what you build.
Now

Right now I'm focused on growing the Bidwell Capital Fund, improving operational systems across the Penn Properties portfolio, and recording new episodes of the Wealth Independence Podcast. I'm also spending more time thinking about how to structure businesses around the life you actually want, not the other way around.

Last updated: Q1 2026
